title: Versioning and Effective Dating Applied Cross-Entity — Plans, Rules, Requirements, and Rates
description: The versioning and effective dating principle that applies to plans (REQ-VER-001–015) shall be consistently applied across all five major entity types: plans, eligibility rules, AI requirements, premium rate tables, and employer contribution configurations. A change to any of these creates a new version, never overwrites the current record.

title: Eligibility Rule Versions: Changes Create New Draft — Published Rules Are Immutable
description: Published eligibility rules are immutable. Any change to a published rule (threshold, condition, effective date) creates a new DRAFT version. The published version remains active until the new version is approved and published. In-flight enrollment sessions referencing the old rule are flagged.

title: AI Requirements Versioned: Original AI Text Preserved When Human Edits
description: When a human reviewer edits an AI-generated requirement text, the original AI-generated text is preserved in the version history. The current version shows the human-edited text. The audit trail records both versions and the reviewer's identity.

title: Effective Dating: Every Configuration Change Has a Future Effective Date
description: Configuration changes (rate table updates, rule threshold changes, contribution % changes) shall support a future effective date. The system evaluates the version in effect at any given point in time based on the effective date, not the record creation date.
effectiveDateScenario: PPO-500 rate change effective July 1, 2027 → two versions: v1 (Jan 1–Jun 30) and v2 (Jul 1+). Enrollments on June 15 use v1 rates; enrollments on July 5 use v2 rates.

title: Historical Enrollment Always Evaluated Against Configuration Version at Enrollment Date
description: When calculating premiums, eligibility, or contribution for a historical enrollment, the system shall use the configuration version that was in effect at the enrollment date — not the current version. This ensures retroactive accuracy for audit and payroll reconciliation.

title: Premium Rate Table Versioned Per Plan Year — Mid-Year Rates Supported
description: Premium rate tables are versioned per plan year and support mid-year rate changes. The demo uses plan year 2027 with a single rate version. The data model shall support multiple rate versions within a plan year (e.g., v1 Jan–Jun, v2 Jul–Dec).

title: Carrier Mapping Versioned — Carrier ID Changes Do Not Break Historical Records
description: Carrier member ID mappings (benefitsPersonId → carrierId) are versioned with effective dates. If a carrier changes the member ID format or the mapping is corrected, a new version is created. Historical transactions reference the mapping version in effect at the transaction date.

title: Version History Accessible on All Versioned Entities via Version History Panel
description: Every versioned entity shall have a Version History panel accessible from the detail view. The panel shows: version number, status, effective date, changed by, change summary, and a diff view comparing with the previous version.

title: Retired Entities Retained for Historical Traceability — No Hard Delete
description: Retired plans, superseded eligibility rules, deprecated AI requirements, and outdated rate tables shall never be hard-deleted. They are archived with RETIRED/SUPERSEDED/DEPRECATED status. Historical enrollments, audit records, and carrier transactions that reference these versions continue to resolve correctly.

title: Version Diff View Shows Field-Level Before/After for Configuration Changes
description: The version diff view shall show field-level before/after values for configuration changes: which fields changed, old value, new value, and (for AI-driven changes) the requirement source that drove the change.

title: Employer Contribution Rules Versioned Per Plan and Per Effective Period
description: Employer contribution percentages (or flat amounts) shall be versioned per plan and per effective period. The premium calculation engine reads the contribution version effective at the enrollment date.

title: Payroll Mapping Configuration Versioned — Deduction Code Changes Traceable
description: Payroll deduction codes, pay frequencies, and plan-to-deduction-code mappings shall be versioned with effective dates. If a payroll system changes its deduction code for a plan, the new mapping is created as a new version; historical transactions reference the correct prior version.
